queries.code: WITH sales_kpi AS (SELECT calculationperiod, kpivalue FROM Table_0), marketing_kpi AS (SELECT calculationperiod, kpivalue FROM Table_1), customer_service_kpi AS (SELECT calculationperiod, kpivalue FROM Table_2), product_development_kpi AS (SELECT calculationperiod, kpivalue FROM Table_3), logistics_kpi AS (SELECT calculationperiod, kpivalue FROM Table_4), operations_kpi AS (SELECT calculationperiod, kpivalue FROM Table_5) SELECT AVG(sales_kpi.kpivalue) AS avg_sales_kpi, AVG(marketing_kpi.kpivalue) AS avg_marketing_kpi, AVG(customer_service_kpi.kpivalue) AS avg_customer_service_kpi, AVG(product_development_kpi.kpivalue) AS avg_product_development_kpi, AVG(logistics_kpi.kpivalue) AS avg_logistics_kpi, AVG(operations_kpi.kpivalue) AS avg_operations_kpi FROM sales_kpi JOIN marketing_kpi ON LOWER(sales_kpi.calculationperiod) = LOWER(marketing_kpi.calculationperiod) JOIN customer_service_kpi ON LOWER(marketing_kpi.calculationperiod) = LOWER(customer_service_kpi.calculationperiod) JOIN product_development_kpi ON LOWER(customer_service_kpi.calculationperiod) = LOWER(product_development_kpi.calculationperiod) JOIN logistics_kpi ON LOWER(product_development_kpi.calculationperiod) = LOWER(logistics_kpi.calculationperiod) JOIN operations_kpi ON LOWER(logistics_kpi.calculationperiod) = LOWER(operations_kpi.calculationperiod)

queries.code: SELECT t0.entityid, AVG(t0.laeq) AS avg_noise_level, AVG(t1.laeq_den) AS avg_noise_level_den FROM Table_0 t0 JOIN Table_1 t1 ON LOWER(t0.recvtime) = LOWER(t1.recvtime) AND LOWER(t0.dateobserved) = LOWER(t1.dateobserved) GROUP BY t0.entityid

tables.name: Table_0
tables.reason: Contributes the average noise level for each area

tables.name: Table_1
tables.reason: Provides additional noise level data for comparison

queries.response: The average noise level in different areas of the city varies, with some areas having higher levels of noise pollution than others. The query provides a useful comparison of noise levels across different areas, allowing for targeted interventions to reduce noise pollution. By analyzing the average noise level for each area, city planners can identify areas that require more attention and develop strategies to mitigate noise pollution.
queries.translated_response: El nivel de ruido promedio en diferentes áreas de la ciudad varía, con algunas áreas que tienen niveles más altos de contaminación acústica que otras. La consulta proporciona una comparación útil de los niveles de ruido en diferentes áreas, lo que permite intervenciones dirigidas para reducir la contaminación acústica. Al analizar el nivel de ruido promedio para cada área, los planificadores urbanos pueden identificar áreas que requieren más atención y desarrollar estrategias para mitigar la contaminación acústica.
queries.judge_feedback: The query is approved because it directly addresses the question requirements and provides meaningful results. The complexity of the query is justified by the need to compare noise levels across different areas and calculate average noise levels.

queries.question: How do the noise levels in different areas of the city change over time, and what is the overall trend in the city's noise level?
queries.translated_question: ¿Cómo cambian los niveles de ruido en diferentes áreas de la ciudad con el tiempo, y cuál es la tendencia general en el nivel de ruido de la ciudad?
queries.code: WITH noise_trend AS (SELECT t1.laeq_den AS area_noise, t0.laeq AS city_noise, t0.dateobserved AS date FROM Table_0 t0 JOIN Table_1 t1 ON LOWER(t0.dateobserved) = LOWER(t1.dateobserved) JOIN Table_2 t2 ON LOWER(t1.dateobserved) = LOWER(t2.dateobserved)) SELECT area_noise, date, city_noise, LAG(city_noise) OVER (PARTITION BY area_noise ORDER BY date) AS prev_noise FROM noise_trend ORDER BY date

queries.question: Which locations have the highest average noise levels during weekdays and weekends?

queries.question: What is the average KPI value for each calculation period in the sales data, and how does it compare to the average KPI value for the same period in the marketing data, considering only the periods where the KPI value is greater than 0.5?
queries.translated_question: ¿Cuál es el valor promedio de KPI para cada período de cálculo en los datos de ventas, y cómo se compara con el valor promedio de KPI para el mismo período en los datos de marketing, considerando solo los períodos donde el valor de KPI es mayor que 0.5?
queries.code: WITH sales_data AS (SELECT calculationperiod, AVG(kpivalue) AS avg_kpivalue FROM Table_0 WHERE kpivalue > 0.5 GROUP BY calculationperiod), marketing_data AS (SELECT calculationperiod, AVG(kpivalue) AS avg_kpivalue FROM Table_1 WHERE kpivalue > 0.5 GROUP BY calculationperiod) SELECT sales_data.calculationperiod, sales_data.avg_kpivalue, marketing_data.avg_kpivalue FROM sales_data JOIN marketing_data ON LOWER(sales_data.calculationperiod) = LOWER(marketing_data.calculationperiod)

tables.reason: This table is needed to analyze the usage patterns in the system.
